title: chrysodium quoyanum
intro: Chrysodium quoyanum, also known as the Mountain Club Moss or Quoy's Chrysodium, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Gleicheniaceae. It is native to Australia and New Zealand, and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing pain and promoting wound healing. Chrysodium quoyanum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
